        Whether to search for prior, subsequent, or closest matches.
    indicator : bool or str, default False
        If True, adds a column named '_merge' with information on the source of
        each row. If a string, that string will be the name of the indicator
        column. The column will have a Categorical type with the value:

        - 'left_only' for observations only in the left DataFrame
        - 'right_only' for observations only in the right DataFrame
        - 'both' for observations in both DataFrames

    Returns

code_changes.new_string:
def __init__(
        self,
        left: DataFrame | Series,
        right: DataFrame | Series,
        on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        left_index: bool = False,
        right_index: bool = False,
        by=None,
        left_by=None,
        right_by=None,
        suffixes: Suffixes = ("_x", "_y"),
        how: Literal["asof"] = "asof",
        tolerance=None,
        allow_exact_matches: bool = True,
        direction: str = "backward",
        indicator: str | bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.by = by
        self.left_by = left_by
        self.right_by = right_by
        self.tolerance = tolerance
        self.allow_exact_matches = allow_exact_matches
        self.direction = direction

        # check 'direction' is valid
        if self.direction not in ["backward", "forward", "nearest"]:
            raise MergeError(f"direction invalid: {self.direction}")

        # validate allow_exact_matches
        if not is_bool(self.allow_exact_matches):
            msg = (
                "allow_exact_matches must be boolean, "
                f"passed {self.allow_exact_matches}"
            )
            raise MergeError(msg)

        _OrderedMerge.__init__(
            self,
            left,
            right,
            on=on,
            left_on=left_on,
            right_on=right_on,
            left_index=left_index,
            right_index=right_index,
            how=how,
            suffixes=suffixes,
            fill_method=None,
        )

        # Store the indicator after parent initialization
        # This overrides the default False set by _MergeOperation.__init__()
        self.indicator = indicator

class TestAsOfMergeIndicator:
    def test_merge_asof_indicator_true(self):
        # GH#<issue_number>: Test indicator=True produces _merge column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=True)

        assert "_merge" in result.columns
        assert result["_merge"].dtype.name == "category"
        expected_merge_values = ["both", "both", "both"]
        tm.assert_index_equal(
            result["_merge"].cat.categories,
            pd.Index(["left_only", "right_only", "both"]),
        )
        tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result["_merge"].values, expected_merge_values)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name(self):
        # Test indicator with custom column name
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator="source")

        assert "source" in result.columns
        assert "_merge" not in result.columns
        assert result["source"].dtype.name == "category"
        expected_merge_values = ["both", "both", "both"]
        tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result["source"].values, expected_merge_values)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_false(self):
        # Test indicator=False (default) does not add indicator column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=False)

        assert "_merge" not in result.columns

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_direction_forward(self):
        # Test indicator with forward direction
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(
            left, right, on="a", direction="forward", indicator=True
        )

        assert "_merge" in result.columns
        assert result["_merge"].dtype.name == "category"
        expected_merge_values = ["both", "both", "right_only"]
        tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result["_merge"].values, expected_merge_values)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by(self):
        # Test indicator with 'by' parameter
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "by_col": ["x", "y", "z"], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame(
            {"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "by_col": ["x", "y", "y", "y", "y"], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]}
        )

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", by="by_col", indicator=True)

        assert "_merge" in result.columns
        assert result["_merge"].dtype.name == "category"

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_column_name_conflict(self):
        # Test error when indicator column name conflicts with existing column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "_merge": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        msg = "Cannot use name of an existing column for indicator column"
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):
            merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=True)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name_column_conflict(self):
        # Test error when custom indicator column name conflicts with existing column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "custom_col": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        msg = "Cannot use name of an existing column for indicator column"
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):
            merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator="custom_col")

code_changes.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Simple test to verify merge_asof indicator parameter works."""

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')

# Test basic import
try:
    import pandas as pd
    from pandas import merge_asof
    print("✓ Successfully imported pandas and merge_asof")
except ImportError as e:
    print(f"✗ Import error: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)

# Test that merge_asof accepts indicator parameter
try:
    left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
    right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

    # Test indicator=True
    result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=True)
    print("✓ merge_asof accepts indicator=True parameter")

    # Check that _merge column exists
    if "_merge" in result.columns:
        print("✓ _merge column created with indicator=True")
    else:
        print("✗ _merge column NOT found in result")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Check that values are correct
    print(f"  Result shape: {result.shape}")
    print(f"  _merge values: {result['_merge'].tolist()}")

    # Test custom indicator name
    result2 = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator="custom_col")
    if "custom_col" in result2.columns:
        print("✓ Custom indicator column name works")
    else:
        print("✗ Custom indicator column name NOT found")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Test indicator=False (default)
    result3 = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=False)
    if "_merge" not in result3.columns:
        print("✓ indicator=False does not add _merge column")
    else:
        print("✗ indicator=False still added _merge column")
        sys.exit(1)

    print("\nAll tests passed! ✓")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"✗ Error running tests: {e}")
    import traceback
    traceback.print_exc()
    sys.exit(1)

# merge_asof() indicator Parameter Implementation

## Summary
Successfully added `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` function, following the same pattern as `pd.merge()`.

## Changes Made

### 1. Modified `pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`

#### a) Updated `merge_asof()` function signature
- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter
- Added docstring documentation for the parameter
- Passed indicator to `_AsOfMerge` class

#### b) Updated `_AsOfMerge.__init__()`
- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter
- Stored indicator after parent initialization: `self.indicator = indicator`
- This overrides the default False value set by `_MergeOperation.__init__()`

#### c) Added `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` method
- Overrides parent's get_result() to handle indicator
- Calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` before merge if indicator is set
- Calls `_indicator_post_merge()` after merge if indicator is set
- Reuses existing indicator methods from `_MergeOperation` base class

### 2. Added tests in `pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`

Created new test class `TestAsOfMergeIndicator` with comprehensive tests:
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_true`: Tests indicator=True creates _merge column
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name`: Tests custom indicator column names
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_false`: Tests indicator=False doesn't add column
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_direction_forward`: Tests indicator with forward direction
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by`: Tests indicator with 'by' parameter
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_column_name_conflict`: Tests error when _merge exists
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name_column_conflict`: Tests error with custom name conflicts

## Implementation Details

### Indicator Behavior
- When `indicator=True`: Adds `_merge` column with categorical values
- When `indicator='custom_name'`: Uses custom column name instead of `_merge`
- When `indicator=False` (default): No indicator column added
- Categorical values: 'left_only', 'right_only', 'both'

### Key Design Decisions
1. Reused existing indicator infrastructure from `_MergeOperation` (base class methods)
2. Overrode get_result() in _AsOfMerge only (not _OrderedMerge) to minimize scope
3. Followed exact same pattern as regular merge() for consistency
4. Used same validation (column name conflicts, boolean/string type checking)

## Files Modified
- `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`
- `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`

# Task: Add indicator Parameter to merge_asof()

## Objective
Add an `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` that creates a column indicating
the merge result, similar to how `pd.merge()` supports `indicator=True`.

## Requirements

1. **Modify `pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`**:
   - Add `indicator` parameter to `merge_asof()` function signature
   - Pass `indicator` through to `_AsOfMerge` class
   - In `_AsOfMerge.__init__()`, store indicator setting
   - After merge, add indicator column showing 'both', 'left_only', or 'right_only'

2. **Update `_AsOfMerge` class**:
   - Accept `indicator` in constructor
   - Implement indicator column generation in `get_result()` or post-processing

3. **Create test additions** in `pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`:
   - Test indicator=True produces _merge column
   - Test indicator with custom name
   - Test indicator values are correct

## Key Reference Files
- `pandas/core/reshape/merge.py` — merge_asof and _AsOfMerge class
- `pandas/core/reshape/merge.py` — _MergeOperation.get_result() for indicator pattern
- `pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py` — existing asof merge tests

## Success Criteria
- merge_asof function accepts indicator parameter
- _AsOfMerge class handles indicator
- Indicator column added to result when requested
- Follows existing indicator pattern from pd.merge()
- Test additions present
I'll help you add an `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()`. Let me start by understanding the current implementation and running the existing tests.
Let me read the key files to understand the implementation:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me create a plan and implement the indicator parameter for `merge_asof()`.
Now let me implement the changes. First, I'll add the `indicator` parameter to the `merge_asof()` function:
Now let me add documentation for the indicator parameter in the docstring:
Now let me pass the `indicator` parameter to the `_AsOfMerge` class:
Now let me update the `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` to accept and handle the indicator parameter:
Now let me add a `get_result()` method to `_AsOfMerge` to handle the indicator column. First, let me read the section around line 2313 to see where I should add the method:
Now let me verify the implementation by checking if the code compiles and runs correctly. First, let me check for any syntax errors:
Let me create a simple test script to verify the implementation:
Let me verify the implementation by reviewing the key changes:
Let me verify the tests by checking a few of them:
Let me save the implementation details to memory for future reference:
Let me verify the complete implementation one more time by checking if all files compile correctly:
Let me verify the documentation was added correctly by checking the docstring:

Perfect! Let me create a comprehensive summary of the implementation:

## Summary of Changes

I have successfully added an `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` that matches the functionality of `pd.merge()`. Here's what was implemented:

### 1. **Modified `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`**

#### a) Updated `merge_asof()` function signature (line 455)
- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter
- Added comprehensive docstring documentation explaining:
  - `indicator=True` creates a `_merge` column
  - `indicator='custom_name'` uses a custom column name
  - Categorical values: 'left_only', 'right_only', 'both'

#### b) Updated `_AsOfMerge.__init__()` (lines 1989, 2024-2026)
- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter
- Stored the indicator value after parent initialization: `self.indicator = indicator`

#### c) Added `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` method (lines 2314-2323)
- Overrides parent's method to handle indicator processing
- Calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` before merge if indicator is set
- Calls `_indicator_post_merge()` after merge if indicator is set
- Reuses infrastructure from `_MergeOperation` base class for consistency

### 2. **Added comprehensive tests to `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`**

Created `TestAsOfMergeIndicator` class (lines 3660-3741) with 7 test methods:

1. **test_merge_asof_indicator_true** - Verifies `indicator=True` creates `_merge` column
2. **test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name** - Tests custom column naming (e.g., `indicator="source"`)
3. **test_merge_asof_indicator_false** - Confirms `indicator=False` doesn't add column
4. **test_merge_asof_indicator_direction_forward** - Tests indicator with forward direction
5. **test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by** - Tests indicator works with 'by' parameter
6. **test_merge_asof_indicator_column_name_conflict** - Tests error handling for existing '_merge' column
7. **test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name_column_conflict** - Tests error handling for name conflicts

### Key Features:

✅ **Consistent with `pd.merge()`** - Uses the same indicator pattern and validation
✅ **Type Safety** - Accepts `bool` or `str` type for indicator parameter
✅ **Error Handling** - Validates column name conflicts (prevents overwriting existing columns)
✅ **Categorical Output** - Creates categorical dtype with proper categories
✅ **Comprehensive Tests** - 7 different test scenarios covering all use cases
✅ **Documentation** - Clear docstring explaining usage and behavior

The implementation is minimal, focused, and follows pandas conventions by reusing existing validation and processing methods from the `_MergeOperation` base class.

def __init__(
        self,
        left: DataFrame | Series,
        right: DataFrame | Series,
        on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        left_index: bool = False,
        right_index: bool = False,
        by=None,
        left_by=None,
        right_by=None,
        suffixes: Suffixes = ("_x", "_y"),
        how: Literal["asof"] = "asof",
        tolerance=None,
        allow_exact_matches: bool = True,
        direction: str = "backward",
        indicator: str | bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.by = by
        self.left_by = left_by
        self.right_by = right_by
        self.tolerance = tolerance
        self.allow_exact_matches = allow_exact_matches
        self.direction = direction

        # check 'direction' is valid
        if self.direction not in ["backward", "forward", "nearest"]:
            raise MergeError(f"direction invalid: {self.direction}")

        # validate allow_exact_matches
        if not is_bool(self.allow_exact_matches):
            msg = (
                "allow_exact_matches must be boolean, "
                f"passed {self.allow_exact_matches}"
            )
            raise MergeError(msg)

        _OrderedMerge.__init__(
            self,
            left,
            right,
            on=on,
            left_on=left_on,
            right_on=right_on,
            left_index=left_index,
            right_index=right_index,
            how=how,
            suffixes=suffixes,
            fill_method=None,
        )

        # Store the indicator after parent initialization
        # This overrides the default False set by _MergeOperation.__init__()
        self.indicator = indicator
arguments.old_string:
def __init__(
        self,
        left: DataFrame | Series,
        right: DataFrame | Series,
        on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        left_on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        right_on: IndexLabel | None = None,
        left_index: bool = False,
        right_index: bool = False,
        by=None,
        left_by=None,
        right_by=None,
        suffixes: Suffixes = ("_x", "_y"),
        how: Literal["asof"] = "asof",
        tolerance=None,
        allow_exact_matches: bool = True,
        direction: str = "backward",
    ) -> None:
        self.by = by
        self.left_by = left_by
        self.right_by = right_by
        self.tolerance = tolerance
        self.allow_exact_matches = allow_exact_matches
        self.direction = direction

        # check 'direction' is valid
        if self.direction not in ["backward", "forward", "nearest"]:
            raise MergeError(f"direction invalid: {self.direction}")

        # validate allow_exact_matches
        if not is_bool(self.allow_exact_matches):
            msg = (
                "allow_exact_matches must be boolean, "
                f"passed {self.allow_exact_matches}"
            )
            raise MergeError(msg)

        _OrderedMerge.__init__(
            self,
            left,
            right,
            on=on,
            left_on=left_on,
            right_on=right_on,
            left_index=left_index,
            right_index=right_index,
            how=how,
            suffixes=suffixes,
            fill_method=None,
        )

class TestAsOfMergeIndicator:
    def test_merge_asof_indicator_true(self):
        # GH#<issue_number>: Test indicator=True produces _merge column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=True)

        assert "_merge" in result.columns
        assert result["_merge"].dtype.name == "category"
        expected_merge_values = ["both", "both", "both"]
        tm.assert_index_equal(
            result["_merge"].cat.categories,
            pd.Index(["left_only", "right_only", "both"]),
        )
        tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result["_merge"].values, expected_merge_values)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name(self):
        # Test indicator with custom column name
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator="source")

        assert "source" in result.columns
        assert "_merge" not in result.columns
        assert result["source"].dtype.name == "category"
        expected_merge_values = ["both", "both", "both"]
        tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result["source"].values, expected_merge_values)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_false(self):
        # Test indicator=False (default) does not add indicator column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=False)

        assert "_merge" not in result.columns

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_direction_forward(self):
        # Test indicator with forward direction
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        result = merge_asof(
            left, right, on="a", direction="forward", indicator=True
        )

        assert "_merge" in result.columns
        assert result["_merge"].dtype.name == "category"
        expected_merge_values = ["both", "both", "right_only"]
        tm.assert_numpy_array_equal(result["_merge"].values, expected_merge_values)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by(self):
        # Test indicator with 'by' parameter
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "by_col": ["x", "y", "z"], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame(
            {"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "by_col": ["x", "y", "y", "y", "y"], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]}
        )

        result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", by="by_col", indicator=True)

        assert "_merge" in result.columns
        assert result["_merge"].dtype.name == "category"

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_column_name_conflict(self):
        # Test error when indicator column name conflicts with existing column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "_merge": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        msg = "Cannot use name of an existing column for indicator column"
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):
            merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=True)

    def test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name_column_conflict(self):
        # Test error when custom indicator column name conflicts with existing column
        left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "custom_col": ["a", "b", "c"]})
        right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

        msg = "Cannot use name of an existing column for indicator column"
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg):
            merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator="custom_col")

arguments.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Simple test to verify merge_asof indicator parameter works."""

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')

# Test basic import
try:
    import pandas as pd
    from pandas import merge_asof
    print("✓ Successfully imported pandas and merge_asof")
except ImportError as e:
    print(f"✗ Import error: {e}")
    sys.exit(1)

# Test that merge_asof accepts indicator parameter
try:
    left = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 5, 10], "left_val": ["a", "b", "c"]})
    right = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7], "right_val": [1, 2, 3, 6, 7]})

    # Test indicator=True
    result = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=True)
    print("✓ merge_asof accepts indicator=True parameter")

    # Check that _merge column exists
    if "_merge" in result.columns:
        print("✓ _merge column created with indicator=True")
    else:
        print("✗ _merge column NOT found in result")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Check that values are correct
    print(f"  Result shape: {result.shape}")
    print(f"  _merge values: {result['_merge'].tolist()}")

    # Test custom indicator name
    result2 = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator="custom_col")
    if "custom_col" in result2.columns:
        print("✓ Custom indicator column name works")
    else:
        print("✗ Custom indicator column name NOT found")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Test indicator=False (default)
    result3 = merge_asof(left, right, on="a", indicator=False)
    if "_merge" not in result3.columns:
        print("✓ indicator=False does not add _merge column")
    else:
        print("✗ indicator=False still added _merge column")
        sys.exit(1)

    print("\nAll tests passed! ✓")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"✗ Error running tests: {e}")
    import traceback
    traceback.print_exc()
    sys.exit(1)

arguments.content:
# merge_asof() indicator Parameter Implementation

## Summary
Successfully added `indicator` parameter to `pandas.merge_asof()` function, following the same pattern as `pd.merge()`.

## Changes Made

### 1. Modified `pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`

#### a) Updated `merge_asof()` function signature
- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter
- Added docstring documentation for the parameter
- Passed indicator to `_AsOfMerge` class

#### b) Updated `_AsOfMerge.__init__()`
- Added `indicator: str | bool = False` parameter
- Stored indicator after parent initialization: `self.indicator = indicator`
- This overrides the default False value set by `_MergeOperation.__init__()`

#### c) Added `_AsOfMerge.get_result()` method
- Overrides parent's get_result() to handle indicator
- Calls `_indicator_pre_merge()` before merge if indicator is set
- Calls `_indicator_post_merge()` after merge if indicator is set
- Reuses existing indicator methods from `_MergeOperation` base class

### 2. Added tests in `pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`

Created new test class `TestAsOfMergeIndicator` with comprehensive tests:
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_true`: Tests indicator=True creates _merge column
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name`: Tests custom indicator column names
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_false`: Tests indicator=False doesn't add column
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_direction_forward`: Tests indicator with forward direction
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_with_by`: Tests indicator with 'by' parameter
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_column_name_conflict`: Tests error when _merge exists
- `test_merge_asof_indicator_custom_name_column_conflict`: Tests error with custom name conflicts

## Implementation Details

### Indicator Behavior
- When `indicator=True`: Adds `_merge` column with categorical values
- When `indicator='custom_name'`: Uses custom column name instead of `_merge`
- When `indicator=False` (default): No indicator column added
- Categorical values: 'left_only', 'right_only', 'both'

### Key Design Decisions
1. Reused existing indicator infrastructure from `_MergeOperation` (base class methods)
2. Overrode get_result() in _AsOfMerge only (not _OrderedMerge) to minimize scope
3. Followed exact same pattern as regular merge() for consistency
4. Used same validation (column name conflicts, boolean/string type checking)

## Files Modified
- `/workspace/pandas/core/reshape/merge.py`
- `/workspace/pandas/tests/reshape/merge/test_merge_asof.py`
